_meta.description: Portfolio summary — the synthesizing view: the defining insight, thematic clusters, a four-question framework for any position, and a catalyst calendar by vertical.

defining_insight.title: Own the chokepoints; the bottleneck owners win regardless of which brand wins
defining_insight.body: Across this entire universe, value concentrates at the stages no one can easily replicate: ASML (EUV), TSMC (leading-edge + CoWoS), SK Hynix (HBM), Nvidia/Broadcom (platform + ASIC), plus the quiet upstream Japanese chokepoints (wafers, resist, reducers). The end-products — GPUs vs ASICs, this robot vs that, one glasses brand vs another — are contested and crowded, but they all flow through the same narrow gates. The most durable exposure is upstream at the bottleneck, not downstream at the brand. The dominant structural risk overlaying all of it is the geographic concentration in Taiwan + Korea.

clusters.title: Asia proxies
items: Foxconn / Quanta / Wiwynn — AI server ODM

four_questions.a: Is this a stage only a handful of players can do (ASML, TSMC CoWoS, HBM, reducers) — or a contested, capacity-adds-easily layer (OSAT overflow, transceiver assembly, robot brands)? Pay up for the former; trade the latter on the cycle.

four_questions.q: Who captures value vs who does volume?
four_questions.a: Volume and value often sit in different places: China assembles ~63% of transceivers but US silicon + lasers capture the margin; ASICs may take share but TSMC + HBM win either way. Buy the value-capture layer, not the volume layer.

How concentrated in Taiwan/Korea is it, and is it an export-control target or lever (EDA, WFE, HBM, China stack)? Concentration is both the moat and the tail risk — size accordingly and watch policy on/off switches.

Does demand persist through a hyperscaler capex pause (EDA royalties, mature-node) or does it hinge entirely on AI capex ROI continuing (accelerators, networking, power/cooling)? Match position size to where you sit on that spectrum.

cats: Samsung share recovery vs SK Hynix (12m)
